title: Moved Docs Viewer Header Controls Into The App Shell
summary: Moved Docs Viewer scope picker, recently-added button, search input, and management action mount composition out of the Liquid shell templates and into a focused JavaScript header-controls renderer coordinated by the app shell.
effect: The shared Jekyll and standalone Docs Viewer shells now provide only a route-context mount for the header-control area while preserving the existing control IDs used by config, route, search, bookmark, and management controllers. Public read-only routes continue to omit management UI/JS, and the local manage shell keeps scope switching through the existing config controller.
verification: node syntax checks passed for docs-viewer.js, docs-viewer-app-shell.js, and docs-viewer-header-controls-renderer.js
Python py_compile passed for docs_viewer_service.py and docs_viewer_app_shell_modules.py
public Docs Viewer read-only smoke passed against /tmp/dlf-jekyll-build
focused public Library header/search smoke passed
standalone Docs Viewer service manage smoke passed
Docs Viewer management scope lifecycle UI smoke passed
